name: Real document gold set - summary asap-2025-annual-report-tagged.pdf
input_text: Summarize the selected report for an executive reader. Preserve the human-spaceflight risk view, Artemis and Commercial Crew concerns, acquisition/contracting findings, workforce and technical-authority concerns, and ISS transition risk.
notes: Manual gold set prepared from the ASAP 2025 Annual Report. The evaluator should prefer substantive executive coverage over table-of-contents paraphrase.

expected_executive_summary_signals: Aerospace Safety Advisory Panel Annual Report for 2025
International Space Station transition or deorbit
expected_key_insights_any: Artemis III was assessed as carrying too many first-time objectives or an unbalanced risk posture
commercial contracting and acquisition strategy were framed as an emerging determinant of safety outcomes
technical authority and workforce attrition were raised as significant safety concerns
the ISS was characterized as entering the riskiest period of its operational life
NASA was urged to strengthen the transition to commercial LEO destinations and maintain safe ISS deorbit planning

too many first-of-a-kind objectives are stacked into Artemis III
the panel recommended redistributing objectives across missions

fixed-price and commercial business models can affect risk and oversight

the panel highlighted the need for independent technical authorities
human capital reductions were treated as a risk to safe execution

commercial crew and Starliner highlighted governance and risk-accountability issues
NASA was urged to refine anomalous flight event declaration guidance
